name: codex-shares-guards-deny
doc: Cross-harness invariant: the Codex edition routes through the SAME handleHook → evaluate() guard chain as Claude Code, only the response envelope differs (respond.ts:38-39, codex shares the claude-code branch → hookSpecificOutput.permissionDecision). Two shared guards are exercised over the codex spawn (`hook codex core`): (1) bashWriteGuard heredoc-into-code-file (policy/guards/bash-write.ts) and (2) the destructive-git gate (evaluate.ts:22). A plain read passes. Proves Codex is not enforcement theatre on the Bash primitive. Real output verified 2026-07-06 against `bun src/cli/bin.ts hook codex core`.

steps.doc: Heredoc redirected into a .ts file → bashWriteGuard blockCodeWrite (bash-write.ts:45). Codex emits the claude-code deny envelope (respond.ts:40 denyResponse). Same block as scenario 04 step 1, but spawned as `hook codex core` — the guard is harness-independent.
`git push --force` matches GIT_BLOCKED (patterns.ts:9) → deny "Destructive git command" (evaluate.ts:22-34). Assert the [BLOCKED] tag survives into permissionDecisionReason on the codex envelope.
A plain read command (`ls`): no guard, no git match, no file-size → allow. The `hook` command prints the empty allow (no notice) → empty stdout, exactly like claude-code.
